channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Inside Apple Music: How It Competes Without A Free Tier | Billboard On The Record
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to Billboard On The Record. Today, we're talking to Oliver Schusser, VP of Apple Music and international content, about what sets Apple Music apart from other streaming services.
[Soft music continues in the background]
Host: Oliver, thanks for joining us. We're here at Apple Music's brand new studios in Culver City. Can you tell us about this space and why it's a key part of your strategy?
Oliver Schusser: We built this studio to provide a platform for artists to connect with their fans in a more personal way. When we started Apple Music 11 years ago, we were concerned that music streaming would become impersonal. We wanted to create a space where artists could express themselves beyond just their music.
Host: That's really interesting. So, how does this focus on artist relationships and high-quality content translate to the overall Apple Music experience?
Oliver Schusser: For us, it's about creating a curated, high-quality listening experience that goes beyond just scale. We prioritize editorial expertise, artist relationships, and product integration to make Apple Music stand out.
Host: I see. And what about your approach to discovery and fraud prevention? How do you use technology to support these efforts?
Oliver Schusser: We use AI to help us with discovery and fraud prevention. This allows us to provide a better experience for our users and support our artists and labels.
Host: Lastly, can you tell us about your stance on maintaining a paid-only model and how that affects your partnerships with artists and labels?
Oliver Schusser: Our paid-only model is an important part of our approach. We believe it allows us to prioritize quality and support our artists and labels in a way that's sustainable and fair.
[Outro music starts]
Host: Thanks for tuning in to this episode of Billboard On The Record. If you liked what you heard, be sure to rate, review, and subscribe to our podcast. You can also follow us on Instagram, TikTok, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and YouTube @billboard. We're in partnership with SickBird Productions. Until next time, thanks for listening!
[Outro music continues to play until the end]
